domain: cymatics / acoustics (Chladni-plate nodal-line patterns under standing-wave excitation)
specific_mechanism: A thin metal plate excited at a resonant frequency develops standing waves; loose particles on the plate migrate from antinodes (high vibration amplitude) to nodes (zero displacement), tracing the eigenfunction's nodal set as a visible figure. Different frequencies produce different Chladni figures; the basis is the spectrum of the plate's vibrational modes (Bessel/spherical-harmonic-like). The pattern is purely a function of (geometry, boundary conditions, excitation frequency).
llm_application: Frequency-conditioned representation cartography: treat the attention-pattern map of an LLM at a fixed prompt as a 'plate'; sweep a controllable 'frequency' parameter (a learned scalar inserted into the residual stream as a sinusoidal bias) and record which token positions remain quiescent (low-activation 'nodes') vs which oscillate. The set of quiescent positions for a given frequency forms a 'Chladni figure' that probes the model's mode structure. Phase-coherence across heads is required for a clean nodal set; incoherent heads blur the pattern.
novelty_claim: Spectral / Fourier-feature attention work (FNet 2105.03824, FrequencyAttention CVPR 2023, FreqRise) all use frequency as a feature transform. None inject a sweepable sinusoidal scalar specifically to read off a 'nodal set' of position-wise quiescent activations as a model-mode diagnostic.
